1. **Function $ L(t) $ for LED Light Output:**

To maintain a constant illumination of 500 lumens in the living room, the LED light output $ L(t) $ must compensate for the natural light $ N(t) $. The total illumination is the sum of the natural light and the LED light:

$$
500 = N(t) + L(t)
$$

Solving for $ L(t) $:

$$
L(t) = 500 - N(t)
$$

Substituting the given function for $ N(t) $:

$$
L(t) = 500 - \left(300 + 200\sin\left(\frac{\pi}{12}t\right)\right)
$$

$$
L(t) = 200 - 200\sin\left(\frac{\pi}{12}t\right)
$$

This function ensures that the total illumination is always 500 lumens, regardless of the time of day.

**Total Energy Consumption Over 24 Hours:**

The LEDs consume 0.1 watts per lumen-hour. The total energy consumption is:

$$
\text{Total Energy} = \int_0^{24} L(t) \cdot 0.1 \, dt
$$

Substitute $ L(t) $:

$$
\text{Total Energy} = \int_0^{24} \left(200 - 200\sin\left(\frac{\pi}{12}t\right)\right) \cdot 0.1 \, dt
$$

$$
= 0.1 \int_0^{24} \left(200 - 200\sin\left(\frac{\pi}{12}t\right)\right) \, dt
$$

$$
= 0.1 \left[ 200t + 200 \int_0^{24} \sin\left(\frac{\pi}{12}t\right) \, dt \right]
$$

Compute the integral:

$$
\int_0^{24} \sin\left(\frac{\pi}{12}t\right) \, dt = \left[ -\frac{12}{\pi} \cos\left(\frac{\pi}{12}t\right) \
